title: Pattern Search
subtitle: / searches forward, ? searches back. n and N step through matches.
summary: /pattern searches forward; ?pattern searches backward. n repeats the search in the original direction; N reverses it. Search is one of Vim's most powerful motions.

blocks.text: Vim's search is two-stage: type {key:/} (or {key:?}) at the cursor's current line, type the pattern, press {key:Enter}. Cursor jumps to the first match. {key:n} for the next match, {key:N} for the previous.

The pattern is a *regex*, not a literal string. {key:/}{key:f}{key:o}{key:o}{key:.}{key:Enter} matches `foo` followed by *any* character. To search for a literal `.`, escape it: {key:/}{key:f}{key:o}{key:o}{key:\}{key:.}{key:Enter}. Or use the `\V` (very nomagic) prefix to disable regex meaning altogether: {key:/}{key:\}{key:V}{key:f}{key:o}{key:o}{key:.}{key:Enter}.

blocks.title: Search Is a Motion
blocks.text:
{key:/}{pattern}{key:Enter} is a *motion*: it produces a (start, end) pair just like {key:w} or {key:G}. So every operator pairs with it.

  • {key:d}{key:/}{key:E}{key:N}{key:D}{key:Enter} — delete from here through the next `END`.
  • {key:c}{key:/}{key:fn}{key:Enter} — change from here to the next `fn`.
  • {key:y}{key:?}{key:def}{key:Enter} — yank from here back to the previous `def`.

Search is also a *jump* — it pushes the previous position to the jumplist, so {key:Ctrl-O} returns you. And the pattern itself is stored in the **`/` register** ({key:"}{key:/}), reusable in {key::}{key:s} commands.

blocks.title: Settings That Change How Search Feels
blocks.text:
• `'incsearch'` — show matches as you type. On by default in Neovim.
  • `'hlsearch'` — highlight all matches after Enter. {key::}{key:n}{key:o}{key:h}{key:Enter} clears the highlight without losing the search history.
  • `'ignorecase'` — case-insensitive search.
  • `'smartcase'` — combined with `ignorecase`, search is case-insensitive *unless* you typed a capital letter. The pragmatic default.
  • `'wrapscan'` — when the search hits the end of file, wrap to the top. On by default; turn off if you want hard stops.

The pattern syntax has four "magic" levels: `\v` (very magic — most regex chars are special), default `\m`, `\M` (nomagic), `\V` (very nomagic — almost everything literal). For complex patterns prefer `\v`.
Empty pattern reuses the previous one: {key:/}{key:Enter} jumps to the next match of whatever you last searched for.

rows: Jump back / forward (search jumps land here)
